TableData.TableDescription: These options enable the various shift pattern modes within the PCM. 'None' = Performance mode via a switch is not functional. 'Performance Switch' = Performance mode via a switch can be activated
This should never be touched unless you change your transmission type from a 4l60e to 4l80e.

If this parameter is Manual then the transmission is a manual gear, If it is Automatic then it is an automatic transmission. Note: This calibration should only be used as a reference, it is recommended you do not alter the value. Only the following values may be entered into this table: - Automatic - Manual

TableData.RowHeaders: 2wd Torque Limit 1st Gear,2wd Torque Limit 2nd Gear,2wd Torque Limit 3rd Gear,2wd Torque Limit 4th Gear,2wd Torque Limit Reverse,4wd Low Torque Limit 1st Gear,4wd Low Torque Limit 2nd Gear,4wd Low Torque Limit 3rd Gear,4wd Low Torque Limit 4th Gear,4wd Low Torque Limit Reverse,4wd High Torque Limit 1st Gear,4wd High Torque Limit 2nd Gear,4wd High Torque Limit 3rd Gear,4wd High Torque Limit 4th Gear,4wd High Torque Limit Reverse

TableData.TableDescription: If the vehicle speed is above this value, then abuse management is disabled.
If the throttle is above this value "Abuse Management (in Gear) Upper RPM Threshold"), then abuse mode is enabled.
If abuse management is active, then the throttle must fall below this value to disable abuse management

TableData.TableName: Abuse Management In P/N Upper RPM
TableData.TableDescription: Time that the drive train abuse remains enabled.

TableData.TableName: Abuse Management In P/N Lower RPM
TableData.TableDescription: If abuse management is active and the transmission is in P/N, then engine RPM must fall below this value to disable abuse management once active.

TableData.TableName: Abuse Management In Gear Upper RPM
TableData.TableDescription: Time that the drive train abuse remains enabled.

TableData.TableName: Abuse Management In Gear Lower RPM
TableData.TableDescription: If abuse management is active and the transmission is in P/N, then engine RPM must fall below this value to disable abuse management once active.
Time that the drive train abuse remains enabled.

TableData.TableName: Torque Management Min Temp To Enable
TableData.RowHeaders: Torque Management Min Temp To Enable
TableData.TableDescription: Temp of engine has to be higher than this value for TM to become active.

TableData.TableName: Abuse Mode Max Time In Drive
TableData.RowHeaders: Abuse Mode Max Time In Drive
TableData.TableDescription: Max time for Abuse Mode to be active when in drive.

TableData.TableName: Abuse Mode Max Time In Reverse
TableData.RowHeaders: Abuse Mode Max Time In Reverse
TableData.TableDescription: Max time for Abuse Mode to be active when in reverse.
When engine speed (RPM) is greater than this value, transmission abuse management can be enabled. Vehicle speed must also be less than Abuse Mode Speed Enable" to enable abuse mode.
If vehicle speed is greater than this value then transmission abuse management will not be enabled.
This is the percentage of torque reduction to use when in an Abuse mode condition.
The PCM will attempt to reduce the engine torque by this amount upon execution of a 2->1 downshift.
The PCM will attempt to reduce the engine torque by this amount upon execution of a 1->2 upshift.
The PCM will attempt to reduce the engine torque by this amount upon execution of a 1->2 upshift.
Determine the desired 1-2 shift times for the transmission adapt values.
Determine the desired 1-2 shift times for the transmission adapt values.
The pressure offset to be used on the Force Motor Current table axis. Increasing these values firms the shift.
The pressure offset to be used on the Force Motor Current table axis. Increasing these values firms the shift.
Determines how much current is applied to achieve the desired line pressure in relation to transmission temperature.
Determines how much current is applied to achieve the desired line pressure in relation to transmission temperature.
BTM can be active in 4WD low if the vehicle speed is above this value.
BTM can remain active in 4WD low if the vehicle speed is above this value.
Min engine speed to allow Brake Torque Management to become active.
BTM can remain active in 2WD if the vehicle speed is above this value.

Engine RPM must be this much above Idle speed before Brake Torque Management (BTM) can become active. Brake Torque Management (BTM) is used to reduce engine power by cutting cylinders and reducing the pedal opening on ETC vehicles when your foot is held on the brake in gear and the accelerator is pressed (stalling up the vehicle).
Max TPS % allowed to show that vehicle has a closed throttle.
Min TPS % allowed to show that vehicle has a closed throttle.
Rate of applying Torque managment when all perameters have been met.
Max Torque reported thru drivetrain.
BTM max during Idle.
Used for BTM system to set up torque values.
Tells the ecm how much amps to ask for via engine RPMs.

Engine coolant temperature must be above this to enable DFCO. When all of the DFCO enablers are met, hitting any DFCO activate level will turn on DFCO. After DFCO has been enabled hitting any DFCO disabler or hitting every DFCO deactivate will turn off DFCO.
Vehicle speed must be above this to enable DFCO.
Vehicle speed must remain above this to keep DFCO active.
RPM must be above this to enable DFCO.
RPM must remain above this to keep DFCO active.
Manifold pressure must be above this to enable DFCO.
Manifold pressure must remain above this to keep DFCO active.
Provides engine coolant temperature correction to spark timing.
Provides engine coolant temperature correction to spark timing when the throttle is closed.
Provides intake air temperature correction to spark timing.

Any value in "Spark IAT Table" will be multiplied by this amount before being summed in the final spark advance calculation. This table can be used to turn off IAT spark correction when the engine is in any load cell.
Provides fuel mixture correction to spark timing.
Spark retard to assist in catalytic converter light-off on start up.
This table is used as a reference for what would be best timing, which is then used for various torque calculations.
The minimum spark timing permitted at any time, the PCM will not allow spark advance to be reduced below this level.
Provides fuel mixture correction to spark timing multiplication of table B5908 based off of engine load.

Multiplier for the catalytic converter light-off table. * EXAMPLES * 0.0 = No Correction applied from catalytic converter light-off spark table. 1.0 = Use the full value defined in catalytic converter light-off spark table. 0.5 = Use 50% of the value defined in catalytic converter light-off spark table
When in Piston Protection Mode, this is the amount of spark that will be pulled for safety.
Timing values used when the engine is cranking.

TableData.TableName: Minimum Spark Advance Traction Control Active
TableData.TableDescription: The minimum spark timing permitted at any time with traction control active.
Sets the spark retard required to cause the desired engine torque loss.
Min dwell coil charge time.
This is the main dwell table for the coil.
Spark will adjust itself by this amount to try to keep the desired idle.
Spark will adjust itself by this amount to try to keep the desired idle.
Spark will adjust itself by this amount to try to keep the desired idle.
Vehicle Speed threshold for Idle Spark Advance.
Lowest MAP value for octane scaler adjustments.
MAP hysteresis for octane scaler adjustments.
The Max Spark Value allowed by any table.
When RPMs are being limited, the computer will use this spark value.
Spark can not go higher than this amount when in idle mode.
Used to convert effective area (which the PCM calculates from the desired idle airflow) into actual IAC motor steps.
Correction added to the IAC airflow when in drive. The pcm uses this table to correct for the airflow needed if the engine rpm is running too high.
Correction added to the IAC airflow when in park or neutral. The pcm uses this table to correct for the airflow needed if the engine rpm is running too high.
Correction added to the IAC airflow when in Drive. The pcm uses this table to correct for the airflow needed if the engine rpm is running too low.
Correction added to the IAC airflow when in park or neutral. The pcm uses this table to correct for the airflow needed if the engine rpm is running too low.
Minimum vehicle speed required to allow the EGR system to be enabled.

TableData.RowHeaders: Cat Monitor,Cat Heater Monitor,Evap System,Air Pump,AC System,O2 Sensor,O2 Heat,EGR
TableData.TableDescription: Tells the computer what tests are allowed to be run. Only the following values may be entered into this table: 0 = No 1 = Yes
Throttle must not fall this much below "PE Throttle TPS Threshold" to keep Power Enrichment fueling mode enabled.
Bypass the power enrichment delay if above this RPM.
Amount of time the PE (Power Enrichment) conditions must exist before entering into PE (Power Enrichment) fueling mode.

TableData.TableName: Ratio of Air to Fuel for Stoichiometry
TableData.RowHeaders: Ratio of Air to Fuel for Stoichiometry
TableData.TableDescription: The air-fuel ratio (AFR) considered to be stoichiometric. This value is used by the PCM to determine the AFR of EQ ratio 1.0. AFR values for EQ ratios are calculated by dividing this value by the specified EQ ratio.
RPM must be higher than this value to enable Power Enrichment fueling mode.
Throttle % must be greater than this value to enable Power Enrichment fueling mode.
If Power Enrichment fueling criteria have been met yet the delay is still present, this table allows the delay to count down faster based on the throttle position.
Number of cylinders for this engine application.
TableData.ExtraDescription: ** DO NOT CHANGE ***If you need to change this, swap the whole calibration block for a proper one.Defines the number of cylinders in the calibration.

TableData.TableDescription: When the ignition key is first turned to the on position the PCM will deliver a prime pulse of fuel.
After a stall condition, this is the amount of fuel injected into the intake.
Base Spark Advance.
Base Spark Advance,Closed throttle

This is the main table that is used to tune WOT fuel. The values in this table are Fuel/Air multipliers (or Equivalence Ratio) ie. values greater than 1 are richer, values less than one are leaner. The values relate directly to the Stoich AFR and the resulting commanded AFR is Stoich divided by this PE multiplier. Eg. if the multiplier is 1.1 then the commanded AFR will be 14.7/1.1 or 13.36.

TableData.ColumnHeaders: P/N AC Off,P/N AC On,In Gear AC Off,In Gear AC On
TableData.TableDescription: Desired Idle Speeds. Based on various Transmission and Air Conditioner combinations.

Used to determine airmass per cylinder in case of MAF failure and also to provide a base airflow value to check for MAF failure. It is also used to provide transient condition correction to the VCM main airmass calculations
Used in some code variations when MAF failure is detected.
This table defines the MAF measured Air Flow Rates (grams per second) versus the Frequency Output (Hz) to the PCM.
Use to shut off the air pump diagnostics. Should only be changed if removed from vehicle. It helps with Cat light off.

TableData.Values: Enum: 0:1 Trip (MIL IMMEDIATELY),1:2 Trips (MIL if DTC active two drive cycles),2:(No MIL store DTC),3:Not Reported (DTC Disabled)

TableData.TableDescription: Uncheck = No MIL (Lamp always off) Check = MIL (Lamp may be commanded on by PCM)
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
*** DO NOT HAND EDIT *** Displayed for information only!
Shuts the vehicle anti-theft system on and off. When off the PCM can not shut off fuel to the engine if the VATS key has not been sent.
RPM at which the A/C system will be disabled.
Throttle Position at which the A/C system will be disabled.
Throttle Position at which the A/C system will be re-enabled.
Maximum allowed vehicle speed before fuel is shutoff on vehicles without Electronic Throttle Control (ETC).
If the fuel has been shutoff due to the maximum allowed speed being reached, the speed must fall by this much before fuel will be re-enabled.

This value is used to calculate the speed value for the engine routines and gauge cluster. It is the number of pulses received from the VSS sensor per mile/kilometer traveled. If you know your gear, tire and location of your VSS sensor you can calculate this value manually.
Number of pulses of the output shaft sensor per mile/kilometer. Used to control the ABS on some vehicles.
Axle gear ratio for calibration application, Units: Multiplier_0_to_16
TableData.ExtraDescription: This value is used by the torque subsystem to estimate the axle torque.It is not used by the PCM for calculating either the speedometer or the transmission shift speeds.On many vehicles (especially corvettes) it is set incorrectly, usually to the default value of 2.73 regardless of the actual differential ratio.

TableData.TableDescription: The number of trans output shaft revolutions per mile. This value is used to calculate the speed that is used for shift and TCC calculations. (A tire sanity check value is provided alongside so you can check the trans tire size calibration against the speedo tire size calibration. This is also useful for troubleshooting accurate speedo but trans shifting strangely issues)
Fan outputs will be used when A/C is active if enabled.
Shuts on or off A/C system in PCM.
Maximum Fuel Tank volume as rated by GMUTS and/or the platform., Units: Gallons
Fuel Gauge Output vs. Level: This is the PWM duty cycle for the fuel gauge output versus remaining fuel level.
If the fuel level reading is equal to or above this value then it is considered a fault., Units: A/D Counts
If the fuel level reading is equal to or below this value then it is considered a fault., Units: A/D Counts
Fuel Primary Volume % vs. Level: This is the remaining percentage of fuel volume versus remaining fuel level from the sender.
Fuel Primary Gauge Volume % vs. Level: This is the remaining percentage of fuel volume versus remaining fuel level from the sender, used for the fuel gauge.
Cooling Fan will turn on if the engine coolant temperature is above this value.
Cooling Fan will turn off if the engine coolant temperature is below this value.
If AC is on, fan will disable above this speed
If engine coolant temperature is above this value when ignition is switched off, fans will run for Run Time
If AC is on, fan will disable above this speed
Fan enable delay after enable conditions are met.
Minimum time that Fan must be commanded 'on'.
Coolant Temp below which Fan is disabled if ignition is off.
Vehicle speed below which Fan can be enabled due to high coolant temp.
Vehicle speed above which Fan is disabled if enabled by high pressure switch.
Vehicle speed below which Fan can be enabled due to high pressure.
Volume of each cylinder in the engine.
Flow rate of the fuel injectors
Injector Flow compensation based on Fuel Pump Voltage.
This is the Minimum Injector Pulse Width (milliseconds) the PCM can command for a given RPM.
Injector will stay shut for this amount of time when PCM commands no fuel.
Timer for injection compensation due to PCM hardware timing

If the commanded injector on time is less than this value the PCM assumes this to be a 'small pulse', in which case the values from table "Small Pulse Adjust" will also be added onto the final pulse width.
When a very short injector on time is commanded (between 0 - 4mS) the PCM will add the values in this table onto the injector on time.

TableData.RowHeaders: MAF Frequency Fail High,MAF Frequency Fail Low
